headline: Senegal CONFIRMED at 37.5 Developing — May 9 band crossing downgrade holds. First criminal conviction under anti-LGBTQ law (April 10): 6-year sentence — moved law from legislative to judicial reality. UNAIDS: 36% rise in HIV infections since 2010; 79% treatment rate now threatened as law criminalizes HIV support services. 430 USAID-funded community organizations halted. New Humanitarian (May 5): law directly criminalizes HIV infection, hitting healthcare access. International response building: OHCHR High Commissioner urged Senegal not to sign law (March); UNAIDS, Outright International, UN treaty bodies condemning enforcement. Re-assessment confirms 37.5 — the May 9 downgrade evidence remains live and reinforced. Dimensional pressure documented as potentially further downward if enforcement scales (May 2026 enforcement trajectory continuing). No new band crossing this cycle.
boundary_watch_status: Active; downgrade trajectory continues. Distance from Critical floor (20.0): 17.5pt buffer remaining.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3 unchanged. No new AWR signal in cycle window.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 2.5 unchanged. E3 Non-Judgment continues at low level due to legal criminalization of identity.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3 unchanged. Domestic action remains mixed (some healthcare programs continue).
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1.5 unchanged at low level. EQ1 Universality: LGBTQ population legally excluded; PLHIV access criminalized through support-service ban.
scoring_rationale.BND: 2.5 unchanged. B4 Refusal Ethics: refusal-of-care pattern emerging with law enforcement.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2 unchanged. AB1 Harm Acknowledgment: international objection ignored.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3 unchanged. No SYS-level structural change in cycle window.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.5 unchanged. International commitments (UNAIDS partnership, OHCHR engagement) contradicted by law enforcement.

key_evidence: First criminal conviction under anti-LGBTQ law (April 10): 6-year sentence — moved law from legislative to judicial reality.
UNAIDS: 36% rise in HIV infections since 2010; 79% treatment rate threatened.
430 USAID-funded community organizations halted due to US foreign aid freeze (compound healthcare-access negative).
New Humanitarian (May 5): law directly criminalizes HIV infection, hitting healthcare access — multi-dimension negative (EQU + ACT + BND).
OHCHR High Commissioner urged Senegal not to sign law (March).
International response building: UNAIDS, Outright International, UN treaty bodies condemning enforcement.
Senegal continues to engage with international bodies (sign of partial accountability infrastructure).

evidence.source: New Humanitarian — Senegal anti-gay law criminalizes HIV infection, hits services
evidence.finding: Law criminalization of HIV support services hits healthcare access.
First criminal conviction (6-year sentence) — judicial reality.

evidence.source: Outright International — Senegal's new anti-LGBTQ law statement

math_check.validator_check: Confirmation at 37.5 Developing. May 9 band crossing downgrade holds; new evidence reinforces.

negativeSignals: First criminal conviction under anti-LGBTQ law; UNAIDS HIV trajectory; 430 community organizations halted; healthcare access criminalized; international condemnation building.
positiveSignals: Senegal continues to engage with international bodies (UNAIDS, OHCHR) — partial accountability infrastructure. Distinguishes from Critical-band placement.
sector_context: West Africa / civic-space-narrowing cluster. Senegal's band placement at 37.5 is upper Developing — closer to Functional than Critical floor.
open_actions_resolved: May 9 downgrade confirmed at high confidence
open_actions_carried: Watch: USAID-funded organization restoration or further closures
methodology_consistency_check: Confirmation at published score. Methodologically consistent.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
triage_note: Triage 2026-07-21: baseline 37.5 != current published 33.6. World moved since; score-updater would hold. Not applicable.
